title: Boat Hire in Cannes: 2026 Guide to Costs, Seasons & Best Boats
html:
Whether you are visiting the French Riviera for the film festival or a summer holiday, renting a boat in Cannes unlocks the glamour of the Mediterranean. Day trips from the Vieux Port start at around €250 for a small motorboat, and you can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cannes or list your own. Departures typically run from April through October.

Where to set off in Cannes

Cannes offers several well-equipped marinas and natural harbours. The central Vieux Port de Cannes is the most convenient for town access and short trips. Port Pierre Canto, east of the old port, is quieter and often used by larger yachts. Head west to the Rade de Cannes for open-water sailing southward, or anchor in the Baie de Cannes for a calm swim stop. The Îles de Lérins (Sainte-Marguerite and Saint-Honorat) are a quick 20-minute cruise away, offering pine-scented walks and historic fortresses.

Which boat to rent in Cannes

Here are four popular boat types available through BoatHire24. Each listing includes clear pricing and capacity details.

  1. Motor Yacht (Fairline Targa 43), Ideal for a stylish day cruise with up to 10 guests. From €800 per day. The review of this motor yacht shows noted performance and a full sunpad.
  2. Sailing Catamaran (Lagoon 42), Stable and spacious for 8-12 passengers, perfect for families. From €1,200 per day.
  3. Open Speedboat (Axopar 28), Agile for water sports and exploring coves, max 8 guests. From €450 half-day.
  4. Luxury Yacht (Azimut Flybridge), Crewed option for up to 12, with dining and lounging areas. From €2,500 per day.
  5. RIB Tender, Budget-friendly for 6 passengers, no licence needed in many cases. From €300 half-day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cannes?

Prices vary widely by boat type, duration and season.

  • Shared group tours: €80-€150 per person for a 2-3 hour sightseeing trip.
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €250-€450, fuel extra.
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): €1,200-€2,000, often with skipper.
  • Fully crewed luxury yacht: €3,000-€6,000 per day, including crew and meals.

To compare all options, you can rent a boat in Cannes with transparent pricing and verified owners. For the best value, book at least two weeks in advance.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Cannes?

French regulations require a licence for most motorboats over certain power thresholds. For a power (engine) above 6 kW (approximately 8 hp), a ‘permis bateau’ (French boating licence) is needed. However, many hire operators include a qualified skipper or accept an ICC (International Certificate of Competence). Sailboats under 20 metres often have simpler requirements. Always check with the operator, the booking page on BoatHire24 will confirm exact requirements for each vessel.

A local tip: in July and August, the Mistral wind can blow suddenly from the north-west, creating choppy conditions in the Rade de Cannes. Renting a boat with a bimini top or enclosed cabin provides comfortable shade and shelter.

When is the best season for boat hire in Cannes?

The best months are May through September, with peak activity in July and August. The Mistral wind is most frequent in winter and spring, but summer afternoons often enjoy a gentle sea breeze. Many boats are only available from April to October, with limited winter operations. Book in 2026 well ahead for peak weeks to secure your preferred boat.

At a glance

  • Currency: EUR
  • Price range: €250 half-day to €6,000 per day for crewed yachts
  • Peak months: July and August
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours to full day
  • Max capacity: 12 guests (most boats)
  • Distance to Îles de Lérins: 4 nautical miles from Vieux Port

Questions about boat rental in Cannes

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Cannes?

Yes, if you hire a boat with a skipper/crew included, or choose a small boat with engine power under 6 kW; the operator will confirm on your booking.

What is the cancellation policy for boat hire in Cannes?

Policies vary, but most offer free cancellation up to 48-72 hours before departure; check the specific listing terms.

Are pets allowed on board?

Some owners allow pets, but it must be confirmed in advance; the boat listing on BoatHire24 will indicate pet policy.

What happens if the weather is bad?

The captain may cancel or reschedule for safety; you will receive a full refund or alternative date.

To explore 2026 options for your Cannes trip, start with a trusted platform. As a BoatHire24 user, you can also browse the listings to find the perfect boat. If you own a boat in Cannes, consider joining the marketplace to rent it out and generate income.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cannes and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
